votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking if res matches the condition that n is greater than or equal to 8 and n is even.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if res equals the boolean result of n >= 8 and n being even.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if res matches the condition n >= 8 and n being even.

The Coq spec checks if res equals (8 <= n) && (n is even). The Python code checks if res equals (n >= 8) and (n % 2 == 0). Both conditions are logically equivalent: Z.leb 8 n corresponds to n >= 8, and Z.even n corresponds to n % 2 == 0. The andb in Coq corresponds to Python's 'and' for boolean values.

The Coq spec checks if res equals (8 <= n) && (n is even). The Python code checks if res equals (n >= 8) and (n % 2 == 0). Both conditions are logically equivalent: Z.leb 8 n is the same as n >= 8, and Z.even n checks if n is even which is equivalent to n % 2 == 0 in Python for integers. The andb in Coq corresponds to Python's 'and' for boolean values.

The Coq spec checks if res equals (8 <= n) && (n is even). The Python code checks if res equals (n >= 8) and (n % 2 == 0). Both conditions are logically equivalent: Z.leb 8 n corresponds to n >= 8, and Z.even n corresponds to n % 2 == 0. The andb in Coq corresponds to Python's 'and' for boolean values.
Both check that res equals (n >= 8) and n is even; Python int and modulo semantics match Coq Z for parity and order.
Both check that res equals the conjunction of (n >= 8) and evenness of n; Python parity and comparison semantics match Coq Z.leb and Z.even for all integers.
Python returns True iff res equals (n >= 8) and evenness, matching andb (Z.leb 8 n) (Z.even n) for all integers, including negatives.
